data.title: Pump.fun has repurchased over 35% of its total circulating supply of tokens, with a cumulative repurchase value exceeding $360 million.
data.description: According to data monitoring by fees.pump.fun, Pump.fun spent 8,754.98 SOL (approximately $812,000) yesterday to repurchase 421.5 million PUMP tokens. Since starting the PUMP token buyback on July 15, 2025, the cumulative number of PUMP tokens repurchased has reached 125,866,560,136, worth approximately $367,323,928, reducing the total circulating supply by 35.556%.

data.title: Nasdaq-listed Nakamoto launches Bitcoin derivatives program
data.description: Nasdaq-listed Nakamoto announced the launch of its Bitcoin Derivatives Program. Part of its Bitcoin holdings will be held in Kraken's compliant custody arrangement as collateral for derivatives strategies. Bitwise will manage these strategies in a separately managed account (SMA), generating continuous premium income through covered call options and call spread strategies, and hedging against market capitalization volatility caused by falling Bitcoin prices through protective put options and put spread strategies. (Businesswire)

data.title: U.S. Offers $4 Million Reward for Information on Daren Li's Money Laundering Activities
data.description: The U.S. Department of State's Bureau of International Narcotics and Law Enforcement Affairs has announced a reward of up to $4 million for information leading to the arrest of Daren Li, who is involved in money laundering for scam centers in Southeast Asia. According to ChainCatcher, court documents reveal that Daren Li laundered funds obtained from victims through cryptocurrency scams and related fraudulent activities. He directed accomplices to open U.S. bank accounts under shell company names and monitored the interstate and international wire transfers of victim funds. At least $73.6 million was directly deposited into bank accounts associated with him and his accomplices, including at least $59.8 million laundered through U.S. shell companies.

data.title: US law enforcement agencies are offering a $4 million reward for information leading to the arrest of Daren Li, a cryptocurrency money laundering suspect.
data.description: The U.S. State Department’s Bureau of International Narcotics and Law Enforcement Affairs announced a reward of up to $4 million under the Transnational Organized Crime Rewards Program for information leading to the arrest of Daren Li, who assisted various fraud rings in Southeast Asia in laundering their proceeds. According to court documents, Daren Li laundered funds obtained from victims through cryptocurrency scams and related fraudulent activities. He instructed accomplices to open U.S. bank accounts under shell companies and monitored the interstate and international wire transfers of victims’ funds. At least $73.6 million of victims’ funds were directly deposited into bank accounts associated with him and his accomplices, including at least $59.8 million from U.S. shell companies used to launder the proceeds of the fraud.

data.title: Suspected insider trader profits over $2.27 million by opening both long and short positions.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, as APE's price surged by over 110% in a short period, a suspected insider trader profited over $2.27 million by simultaneously establishing long and short positions. On-chain data shows that the address initially deposited 75 ETH (approximately $174,000) as margin to open long and short positions in APE. Subsequently, it purchased 1,027 ETH (approximately $2.37 million) on the HyperLiquid platform and withdrew the funds. Furthermore, it purchased an additional 26 ETH on-chain, totaling 1,053 ETH, ultimately realizing a profit of 978 ETH, equivalent to approximately $2.27 million. This operation, due to its precise timing and unusually high returns, has sparked widespread suspicion of insider trading within the community.

data.title: The Balancer hackers have now exchanged 14,300 ETH for 419.3 BTC.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ember, the Balancer hacker began exchanging ETH for BTC via THORChain yesterday, and has so far exchanged 14,300 ETH for 419.3 BTC (US$32.51 million). He now has 7,700 ETH on the ETH chain and 419.3 BTC on the BTC chain, worth US$50.4 million. He stole assets worth US$98 million last November, so the value has shrunk considerably since ETH was priced around US$3,600 at the time.

data.description: 1. US law enforcement offers a $4 million reward for information leading to the arrest of Daren Li, a cryptocurrency money laundering suspect; 2. US Treasury Secretary Bessenter rules out the possibility of extending waivers for Iranian oil; 3. A US judge dismisses fraud charges against Elon Musk and his founders; 4. Bitmine pledges another 112,040 ETH, worth over $260 million; 5. An account with only a 24% win rate bought $117,000 worth of ETH two hours ago; the Spurs defeated the Trail Blazers in Game 3 of the Western Conference First Round of the NBA playoffs; 6. Aave has received a pledge of 69,576 ETH in aid, and is now only 5,505 ETH short; 7. Polymarket's VP of Engineering: Platform growth far exceeds infrastructure capacity, and performance and architecture upgrades will be promoted; 8. A wallet suspected to be associated with the WorldCoin team deposited 21.177 million WLD into a CEX, worth $5.57 million; 9. A suspected insider trader profited over $2.27 million by opening both long and short positions; 10. The Balancer hackers have now exchanged 14,300 ETH for 419.3 BTC.

data.title: Polymarket Focuses on System Upgrades Amid Rapid Growth
data.description:
Polymarket's new Vice President of Engineering, Josh Stevens, announced on the X platform that the platform's business growth has surpassed the capacity of its current infrastructure. According to Odaily, Stevens noted that the team had previously underinvested in scaling efforts. The engineering team is now prioritizing system upgrades, including optimizing on-chain data latency, advancing chain migration for larger block space and lower gas costs, addressing transaction cancellation issues, improving website responsiveness and user experience, and restructuring the central limit order book (CLOB) system.

Stevens also mentioned that the team is developing a unified TypeScript SDK, a unified API, a new version of Polymarket contracts, and an upcoming perpetual contracts (Perps) system. They are collaborating daily with four security teams to ensure fund safety. He emphasized that weekly engineering updates will be provided and acknowledged that the current engineering capabilities do not match Polymarket's market position, stating, "The coming months will prove everything."

data.title: Singapore's Investment Appeal Strengthens Amid Global Instability
data.description: Singapore's investment prospects have gained traction in light of global instability and conflict, according to a detailed analysis by Bloomberg Intelligence. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the findings in its Singapore Edition newsletter. The report suggests that the current geopolitical climate has enhanced Singapore's attractiveness as a stable investment destination. Investors are increasingly considering Singapore as a safe haven amidst worldwide uncertainties. The analysis underscores Singapore's strategic position and robust economic framework, which continue to draw interest from global investors seeking stability.

data.title: A wallet suspected to be linked to the WorldCoin team deposited 21.177 million WLD tokens into a centralized exchange, worth $5.57 million.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens, two wallets deposited a total of 21.177 million WLD tokens into the cryptocurrency exchange Bybit, worth $5.57 million. These wallets may be associated with the WorldCoin team.

data.title: Aave has received 69,576 ETH in aid pledges, and is now only 5,505 ETH short.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ember, Aave, the lending platform directly affected by the rsETH incident, contributed 25,000 ETH ($57.75 million) to cover bad debts. The total bad debt shortfall is 75,081 ETH. Aave coordinated the establishment of the "DeFi United" program to receive aid funds to fill this gap, and has now received 69,576 ETH in aid commitments (loans and donations), leaving only a 5,505 ETH shortfall.

data.title: Polymarket's VP of Engineering: Platform growth far exceeds infrastructure capacity, which will drive performance and architecture upgrades.
data.description: Josh Stevens, Polymarket's newly appointed VP of Engineering, stated on the X platform that Polymarket's business growth has far exceeded the capacity of its existing infrastructure, and the team had previously underinvested in scaling. The engineering team is currently focusing entirely on system upgrades, including optimizing on-chain data latency, advancing chain migration to obtain larger block sizes and lower gas costs, resolving transaction cancellation issues, improving website responsiveness and user experience, and refactoring the CLOB (Central Limit Order Book) system. Stevens added that the team is also developing a unified TypeScript SDK, a unified API, a new version of Polymarket contracts, and the upcoming perpetual contract (Perps) system, while maintaining daily collaboration with four security teams to ensure fund security. He emphasized that weekly engineering updates will be released in the future, acknowledging that current engineering capabilities have not matched Polymarket's market position, adding, "The next few months will prove everything."

data.title: Bitcoin Needs to Surpass $82,000 to Confirm Bull Market
data.description: Bitcoin must break the $82,000 mark to confirm a full bull market, according to an analysis. This level, previously a support zone, now serves as resistance. According to NS3.AI, the current low trading volume indicates that the uptrend is still in its early stages.

data.title: Aave Faces Bad Debt from rsETH Incident, Seeks Assistance
data.description: In the wake of the rsETH incident, lending platform Aave has been directly impacted, covering 25,000 ETH, equivalent to $57.75 million, to address bad debt. The total bad debt amounts to 75,081 ETH. To bridge this gap, Aave has coordinated the 'DeFi United' initiative to receive aid funds. Blockchain sleuth EmberCN posted on X that the initiative has already secured commitments for 69,576 ETH in loans and donations, leaving a shortfall of 5,505 ETH. Companies like LayerZero have pledged support, although the exact amounts are yet to be confirmed. Aave has also launched a dedicated website for donations and data transparency.

data.title: San Antonio Spurs Shares Purchased for $117,000 in Polymarket's Game 3 Market
data.description: An account recently acquired $117,000 worth of shares supporting the San Antonio Spurs in Polymarket's Western Conference first-round Game 3 market. According to NS3.AI, the average purchase price for these shares was 60 cents.

data.title: China Requires AI Firms to Seek Approval for US Capital
data.description: China has instructed three leading artificial intelligence companies—ByteDance, Moonshot AI, and StepFun—to obtain government approval before accepting capital from the United States. According to NS3.AI, these firms must seek state clearance for any secondary share sales, funding rounds, or other financial transactions involving US-origin money. This move reflects China's increasing scrutiny over foreign investments in its technology sector.

data.title: BitGo COO Highlights Key Controls for Institutional Agentic Finance
data.description: BitGo's Chief Operating Officer, Jody Mettler, emphasized the necessity of implementing four key controls in institutional agentic finance. According to NS3.AI, Mettler identified identity, permissions, policy and approval logic, and auditability as essential safeguards. These measures are crucial as AI agents increasingly participate in trading, payments, and enterprise transactions.

data.title: Bitmine has staked another 112,040 ETH, worth over $260 million.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, Bitmine staked another 112,040 ETH in the past four hours, worth over $260 million.

data.title: An account with a win rate of only 24% bet $117,000 two hours ago on the NBA Western Conference First Round Game 3, in which the Spurs defeated the Trail Blazers.
data.description: According to Odaily Seer's monitoring, in the Polymarket prediction event for "NBA Western Conference First Round Game 3: Spurs vs. Trail Blazers," an account with only a 24% win rate (address: 0x84cfffc3f16dcc353094de30d4a45226eccd2f63) purchased $117,000 worth of bets on the Spurs defeating the Trail Blazers two hours ago, at an average price of 60¢. Game 3 of the NBA Western Conference First Round playoffs between the Spurs and the Trail Blazers will tip off at 10:30 AM Beijing time today. The series is currently tied 1-1. San Antonio Spurs' star player Victor Wahlbanama has been upgraded to questionable status, but is expected to return for Game 3 against the Portland Trail Blazers. Wambanoama was diagnosed with a concussion after hitting his head on the floor early in Game 2. According to the NBA concussion protocol, players cannot fully return to play within 48 hours of an injury, but can begin a gradual return to activity after 24 hours, provided their symptoms do not worsen. Odaily Seer continues to monitor the prediction market, seeing changes before pricing.

data.title: Former Jump Trading Researcher Yiming Zhang Secures Funding from Millennium Management
data.description:
Yiming Zhang, a former senior quantitative researcher at Jump Trading, has secured startup funding from hedge fund giant Millennium Management. According to ChainCatcher, Zhang will operate as an external manager once his non-compete clause expires.

Zhang was a key member of Jump Trading's core strategy department for 17 years. Industry insiders note that Millennium Management has been expanding its influence in the hedge fund sector by providing financial support to external teams, establishing itself as a significant incubator in the field.
